1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.
10	// The schema matches bd/br JSONL format for interchange compatibility.
11	// Unknown fields from external sources are preserved in Extra.
12	type Bead struct {
13		ID            string    `json:"id"`
14		Title         string    `json:"title"`
15		Status        string    `json:"status"`
16		Priority      int       `json:"priority"`
17		IssueType     string    `json:"issue_type"`
18		SchemaVersion int       `json:"schema_version"`
19		Owner         string    `json:"owner,omitempty"`
20		CreatedAt     time.Time `json:"created_at"`
21		CreatedBy     string    `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
22		UpdatedAt     time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
23	
24		// Optional fields (bd-compatible)
25		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
26		Parent      string   `json:"parent,omitempty"`
27		Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
28		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
29		Notes       string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
30	
31		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format
32		Dependencies []Dependency `json:"dependencies,omitempty"`
33	
34		// Extra holds unknown fields for round-trip preservation.
35		// Workflow-specific fields (e.g. HELIX spec-id, execution-eligible)
36		// are stored here and written back on save.
37		Extra map[string]any `json:"-"`
38	}
39	
40	// Dependency represents a link between two beads (bd-compatible format).
41	type Dependency struct {
42		IssueID     string `json:"issue_id"`
43		DependsOnID string `json:"depends_on_id"`
44		Type        string `json:"type"` // "blocks", "related", etc.
45		CreatedAt   string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
46		CreatedBy   string `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
47		Metadata    string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
48	}
49	
50	// BeadEvent records append-only execution evidence.
51	type BeadEvent struct {
52		Kind      string    `json:"kind"`
53		Summary   string    `json:"summary,omitempty"`
54		Body      string    `json:"body,omitempty"`
55		Actor     string    `json:"actor,omitempty"`
56		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
57		Source    string    `json:"source,omitempty"`
58	}
59	
60	// Status constants
61	const (
62		StatusOpen       = "open"
63		StatusInProgress = "in_progress"
64		StatusClosed     = "closed"
65		// StatusBlocked marks an explicit external blocker that can be rechecked.
66		// Dependency waits are derived from dependency status and do not persist as
67		// blocked.
68		StatusBlocked = "blocked"
69		// StatusProposed marks work awaiting operator approval or clarification.
70		// It is outside worker dispatch until transitioned to open or cancelled.
71		StatusProposed = "proposed"
72		// StatusCancelled marks work that will not run and does not satisfy
73		// dependents.
74		StatusCancelled = "cancelled"
75	)
76	
77	// CanonicalStatuses is the single source of truth for the persisted bead
78	// status enumeration. It mirrors the bd/br canonical set documented in
79	// TD-031 §2 and the JSON Schema enum at
80	// cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json. The CI guard tests in
81	// sync_test.go assert that schema, TD doc, and Go source agree with this
82	// list; adding or removing a value here without updating the schema and TD
83	// will fail CI.
84	var CanonicalStatuses = []string{
85		StatusOpen,
86		StatusInProgress,
87		StatusClosed,
88		StatusBlocked,
89		StatusProposed,
90		StatusCancelled,
91	}
92	
93	// IsCanonicalStatus reports whether s is one of the persisted bead statuses
94	// in CanonicalStatuses.
95	func IsCanonicalStatus(s string) bool {
96		for _, c := range CanonicalStatuses {
97			if c == s {
98				return true
99			}
100		}
101		return false
102	}
103	
104	// IssueType constants
105	const (
106		// IssueTypeOperatorPrompt is the bead type used for operator-submitted
107		// prompts that the work runs as instructions.
108		IssueTypeOperatorPrompt = "operator-prompt"
109	)
110	
111	// Default labels and other defaults for operator-prompt beads.
112	const (
113		OperatorPromptLabelKind   = "kind:operator-prompt"
114		OperatorPromptLabelSource = "source:web-ui"
115		// OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance is the auto-AC stub used when an
116		// operator submits a prompt without explicit acceptance criteria. The
117		// structural AC verifier is skipped for operator-prompt beads, so this
118		// stub stands as a human-readable contract only.
119		OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance = "Agent must produce a diff or no_changes rationale; the prompt body is the contract."
120	)
121	
122	// OperatorPromptMutationGuard enforces the no-self-mutation rule from
123	// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create, edit, or
124	// close another operator-prompt bead. The guard returns nil when the
125	// mutation is allowed and a non-nil error when it must be rejected.
126	//
127	// actorIssueType is the issue_type of the bead currently being executed
128	// (empty when no operator-prompt context is active). targetIssueType is
129	// the issue_type of the bead about to be mutated.
130	//
131	// Allow/deny matrix:
132	//
133	//	actor=""               , target=*                → allow
134	//	actor="task"           , target=*                → allow
135	//	actor="operator-prompt", target!="operator-prompt" → allow
136	//	actor="operator-prompt", target=="operator-prompt" → deny
137	func OperatorPromptMutationGuard(actorIssueType, targetIssueType string) error {
138		if actorIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
139			return nil
140		}
141		if targetIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
142			return nil
143		}
144		return fmt.Errorf("bead: operator-prompt bead may not mutate another operator-prompt bead")
145	}
146	
147	// NewOperatorPromptBead constructs a fresh operator-prompt bead from a raw
148	// prompt string, applying the Story 15 template defaults: title is the first
149	// non-empty line of the prompt, full prompt body is preserved verbatim in the
150	// description, default labels are kind:operator-prompt + source:web-ui, the
151	// status starts in `proposed` (approval flow), priority defaults to the
152	// caller-supplied priority (clamped to MinPriority..MaxPriority), and the
153	// acceptance field carries the auto-AC stub.
154	//
155	// The returned bead is not persisted; callers feed it to Store.Create which
156	// will assign the ID and CreatedAt/UpdatedAt timestamps.
157	func NewOperatorPromptBead(prompt string, defaultTier int) *Bead {
158		body := strings.TrimSpace(prompt)
159		title := body
160		if i := strings.IndexByte(body, '\n'); i >= 0 {
161			title = strings.TrimSpace(body[:i])
162		}
163		if title == "" {
164			title = "(empty operator prompt)"
165		}
166		priority := defaultTier
167		if priority < MinPriority {
168			priority = MinPriority
169		}
170		if priority > MaxPriority {
171			priority = MaxPriority
172		}
173		return &Bead{
174			Title:       title,
175			IssueType:   IssueTypeOperatorPrompt,
176			Status:      StatusProposed,
177			Priority:    priority,
178			Labels:      []string{OperatorPromptLabelKind, OperatorPromptLabelSource},
179			Description: body,
180			Acceptance:  OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance,
181		}
182	}
183	
184	// Default values
185	const (
186		DefaultType          = "task"
187		DefaultStatus        = StatusOpen
188		DefaultPriority      = 2
189		DefaultPrefix        = "bx" // used only when repo name detection fails
190		CurrentSchemaVersion = 1
191		MinPriority          = 0
192		MaxPriority          = 4
193	)

196	type StatusCounts struct {
197		Open              int `json:"open"`
198		InProgress        int `json:"in_progress"`
199		Closed            int `json:"closed"`
200		Blocked           int `json:"blocked"`

203		Ready             int `json:"ready"`

205		WorkerReady       int `json:"worker_ready"`
206		DependencyWaiting int `json:"dependency_waiting"`
207		ExternalBlocked   int `json:"external_blocked"`
208		OperatorAttention int `json:"operator_attention"`

210	}

228	type Blocker struct {
229		Kind           string   `json:"kind"`
230		NextEligibleAt string   `json:"next_eligible_at,omitempty"`

232		LastStatus     string   `json:"last_status,omitempty"`
233		LastDetail     string   `json:"last_detail,omitempty"`

243	// DepIDs returns a flat list of dependency IDs for this bead.
244	func (b *Bead) DepIDs() []string {
245		var ids []string
246		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
247			ids = append(ids, d.DependsOnID)
248		}
249		return ids
250	}
251	
252	// HasDep returns true if the bead depends on the given ID.
253	func (b *Bead) HasDep(id string) bool {
254		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
255			if d.DependsOnID == id {
256				return true
257			}
258		}
259		return false
260	}

func TestBeadShowSurfacesFingerprint(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()
	store := factory.beadStore()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Test fingerprint rendering", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	// Append an intake.blocked event with rule_fingerprint
	err = store.AppendEvent(id, bead.BeadEvent{
		Kind:      "intake.blocked",
		Summary:   "operator_required",
		Body:      `{"rule_fingerprint":"fp-12345","outcome":"intake_blocked"}`,
		Actor:     "test",
		Source:    "test",
		CreatedAt: time.Now(),
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, showOut, "IntakeBlocked:", "output should contain IntakeBlocked section")
	assert.Contains(t, showOut, "fp-12345", "output should contain rule_fingerprint")
	assert.Contains(t, showOut, "operator_required", "output should contain outcome")
}

func TestBeadShowSurfacesOperatorOverride(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()
	store := factory.beadStore()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Test override rendering", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	// Append an intake.blocked event with operator_override set to true
	err = store.AppendEvent(id, bead.BeadEvent{
		Kind:      "intake.blocked",
		Summary:   "readiness_failed",
		Body:      `{"rule_fingerprint":"fp-67890","operator_override":true,"outcome":"intake_blocked"}`,
		Actor:     "test",
		Source:    "test",
		CreatedAt: time.Now(),
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, showOut, "IntakeBlocked:", "output should contain IntakeBlocked section")
	assert.Contains(t, showOut, "override: true", "output should distinguish true override")
	assert.Contains(t, showOut, "readiness_failed", "output should contain outcome")

	// Now test with operator_override set to false
	createOut2, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Test false override", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id2 := strings.TrimSpace(createOut2)

		Kind:      "intake.blocked",
		Summary:   "readiness_failed",
		Body:      `{"rule_fingerprint":"fp-99999","operator_override":false,"outcome":"intake_blocked"}`,
		Actor:     "test",
		Source:    "test",
		CreatedAt: time.Now(),
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	showOut2, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id2)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, showOut2, "IntakeBlocked:", "output should contain IntakeBlocked section")
	assert.Contains(t, showOut2, "override: false", "output should distinguish false override")
}
